clinical_history: Pre-operative ECG from a 26 year-old female. What is the diagnosis?
diagnosis: This ECG shows a classic Wolff-Parkinson-White (WPW) pre-excitation pattern. The key triad of findings includes: a short PR interval, a wide QRS complex and delta waves (notching or slurring of the initial portion of the QRS).  Slightly positive delta waves in V1-V3 with a left bundle branch block-appearing morphology most likely indicate a right-sided bypass tract. A prominent physiologic sinus arrhythmia (normal finding) is also noted.  Please see Case #218 and other related cases for more information about WPW patterns
